events.title: Digital Peace of Mind
events.summary: Join 55+ volunteer Barbara Fillip for a two-part technology series! In session 1, learn to declutter files and protect privacy and personal data. In session 2, learn to manage your digital legacy and explore tools like Apple Legacy Contact and Google Inactive Account Manager. No tech experience needed. Attendance at both sessions is recommended. Sponsored by the Alliance for Arlington Senior Prog

events.title: German Conversation
events.summary: Sprechen Sie Deutsch? Join this cordial group of people with knowledge of the language in a weekly dialogue. Stimulating and sociable gatherings provide lively discussions in German on topics that are easy to discuss and of interest to all. No instruction provided.

events.title: Hello Spring Bingo
events.summary: Welcome Spring with a fun game of Bingo! Win prizes, have some light refreshments and enjoy the company of 55+ friends. Bring a guest to learn about our great programs. Your guest does not need a 55+ Pass and does not need to register to attend. Sponsored by the Aurora Hills 55+ Advisory Committee.

events.title: Intergenerational Story Time
events.summary: In partnership with the Aurora Hills Branch Library, 55+ volunteers will share their love of reading and music with children. If you are interested in reading a story out loud during story time, please join us! Books are provided by the Aurora Hills Library. Questions? Please contact 55+ staff at 703-228-5722. Open to all ages: no 55+ Pass required.
events.short_note: Indoor storytime for all ages.

events.title: John Marshall Seed Library

events.short_note: Indoor family-friendly event at John Marshall Library.

events.title: Just Beneath the Surface
events.summary: Looking at the works of three artists, we will take a deep dive into what was happening behind the scenes and beneath the surface of all that paint. Community Arts Programmer Jim Halloran will explain the context, history and techniques of famous (and not so famous) artists. Short demo of an artist’s specific process with discussion to follow.

events.title: Nasa's Shuttle Program
events.summary: Become familiar with NASA’s shuttle program from its inception to its conclusion, including its conception, design, objectives, accomplishments and setbacks. The session will be presented by George Cecchetti, a Kennedy Space Center employee from 1980-1987, and will include his experiences there. This is a hybrid program offered simultaneously in person and virtually.
events.short_note: Workshop.

events.title: Pride Dance Bingo
events.summary: Dance Bingo, created and led by 55+ Center Director Jennifer Weber, is a fun game that combines cardio dancing with the luck of the draw of Bingo. Join us for this special Pride edition featuring anthems from LGBTQ+ icons! You will be sure to sweat and have a good time!
